about.name: 7-Day Oahu Group Trip Itinerary: What Actually Works for Friend Groups
No sign-up required for group members — just share a link
Date polling + itinerary + expense splitting in one place
Free to start — no credit card for core flow
Designed for small friend groups (4–10 people)

mainEntity.name: What is the best time to visit Oahu as a group?

acceptedAnswer.text: April–June offers the best combo of reliable weather, fewer crowds, and manageable prices for group trip planners. September–October is a close second for quiet beaches and easy reservations. Avoid peak July–August and major US holidays for lower stress.

mainEntity.name: How much does a 7-day Oahu group trip actually cost per person?

acceptedAnswer.text: Expect $1,950–$3,150 per person (for four people sharing): split vacation rental, food, activities, group transport, and incidentals included.

mainEntity.name: Do you need a car for an Oahu group trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, especially for North Shore and Kailua days. Rideshare coverage is thin outside Honolulu, and public transit isn’t practical for groups on a schedule.

mainEntity.name: Can you do Hanauma Bay without a reservation?

acceptedAnswer.text: No—Hanauma Bay requires a $25 reservation per non-resident, bookable exactly 48 hours in advance. No walk-up tickets for groups or individuals.

mainEntity.name: Is Oahu family-friendly for multi-age groups?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes—Oahu's beaches, resort areas, and cultural attractions like Bishop Museum and Waikiki Beach work for all ages. Group-friendly facilities are standard.

mainEntity.name: How early do Oahu’s best beaches get crowded?

acceptedAnswer.text: Waikiki fills up from 9 am; North Shore beaches go early on surf days. For Diamond Head or Hanauma Bay, book the earliest entry possible for quiet and parking.

mainEntity.name: What's the best area to stay as a group: Waikiki, Ala Moana, or Kailua?

acceptedAnswer.text: For 4–8 people, Ala Moana vacation rentals offer the best blend of group space, lower price, and central access. Waikiki is best for walkability and nightlife; Kailua is great for low-key groups but requires a car for everything.

mainEntity.name: Which Oahu day trip is best for a group based in Waikiki?

acceptedAnswer.text: A North Shore circuit (Sunset Beach, Pipeline, Waimea Bay, Laniakea turtles) packs the most variety and group-friendly stops for one day. Requires a rental car and an early start.

mainEntity.name: Is Pearl Harbor worth going to with a friend group?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pearl Harbor is a historic must—free tickets for the USS Arizona Memorial, but book timed entry in advance if your group is interested.

mainEntity.name: How do you split costs fairly on a group Oahu trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: Track all expenses in a shared tool or app that logs who paid what—ideally with trip-specific categories (lodging, food, car, activities). Don’t use group chat for IOUs.

@graph.headline: Hawaii Tourism Authority — Oahu
@graph.text: Official Oahu visitor guide from Hawaii Tourism Authority: neighborhoods, beaches, cultural sites, seasonal weather, and visitor guidelines.

@graph.headline: Honolulu Parks & Rec — Hanauma Bay Nature Preserve
@graph.text: Official Hanauma Bay reservation system: $25 non-resident entry, mandatory 2-day advance reservation, closed Mondays and Tuesdays, education video required before swim.

@graph.headline: Hawaii State Parks — Diamond Head State Monument Reservations
@graph.text: Diamond Head reservation system for non-Hawaii residents: $5 entry, timed entry reservations required, summit trail is 0.8 miles one-way with elevation gain.

@graph.headline: Oahu Visitors Bureau — Planning Guide
@graph.text: Oahu Visitors Bureau trip planning resources covering neighborhoods, group travel logistics, seasonal considerations, and regional highlights (North Shore, Windward, South Shore).

@graph.headline: National Park Service — World War II Valor in the Pacific / Pearl Harbor
@graph.text: NPS Pearl Harbor National Memorial: free USS Arizona Memorial program, timed-entry ticket system, separate $ for USS Missouri and Bowfin.
